gap: scheduler — planner authored n2-lineage-transaction and n3-planner-control-plane as exact-file-disjoint siblings, but open-ready admitted only n2 because their broad scripts/plugins areas were not proven safe for concurrent writes; correctness is preserved and n3 must serialize
gap: governed-replay — n4 discovered a narrow n2 API handback after both nodes closed cleanly; reopen-node was used as the documented rare fresh-start path before n5 opened, rotating n2 evidence/baseline while preserving the first completion in ledger/provenance; the new attempt must restate and rerun all prior focused proof plus the handback cases
gap: replan-source-transport — settled attempt n7-code-review:1 produced `repair_requires_replan` with producer slice n2-n6, but repair-node did not persist the authoritative outcome and `kaola-workflow-replan.js prepare` refused `replan_source_outcome_missing`; this live run uses one byte-exact cache bootstrap to reach the planner boundary, and the replacement epoch must make repair outcome persistence a mechanical crash-safe transition before self-hosting can count as end-to-end proof
gap: replan-source-rotation — the second settled review attempt could not persist its source because epoch-1 activation retained the byte-identical active copy of a source already sealed at `.cache/epochs/1/files/.cache/replan-source.json`; after recursive snapshot verification and exact digest equality, this run removes only the active copy as a transparent recovery, and the next child must make two-transition source rotation crash-safe and mechanical
gap: committed-child-integrity — the second bounded prepare refused `replan_activation_integrity_failure` at `child_plan_promoted` because the committed transaction compared the live, normally progressed workflow-plan byte-for-byte with its original all-pending promotion bytes; stored and recomputed author-immutable plan hashes remained equal and the byte diff was confined to ledger/compliance progress, so this live run may use one in-memory guard correction to reach planner dispatch, while the replacement epoch must productize hash-covered integrity, legal runtime-state checks, and durable preservation of the final committed activation receipt before successor rotation
gap: historical-receipt-reinterpretation — after the live-output distinction was corrected in memory, prepare refused at the old fenced-state receipt because the current candidate re-ran its now-modified state writer against a transaction committed by earlier code; the final live state still matched the recorded final receipt byte-for-byte, so the bounded recovery verifies that sealed receipt plus transaction-bound state fields instead of rewriting history, while the replacement epoch must make historical receipt verification versioned/canonical and independent of mutable current implementation semantics
gap: historical-first-node-publication — the byte-exact epoch-2 committed state retained the parent first-node planning fields even though its active plan hash and author-immutable child DAG are valid; the bounded recovery accepts this one historical presentation mismatch only under the committed transaction and final-state receipt, and the replacement epoch must prove every newly promoted state publishes the active child first node and role exactly
gap: epoch3-compliance-bootstrap — after e2 introduced strict current-epoch verification, the live planner-authored runtime compliance table omitted the finalize row and close-node had left pre-seeded e1/e2 rows pending because presence suppressed advancement; main repaired only this hash-excluded table with the already verified evidence bindings, preserving the authored plan hash, while e3 must make pending-row advancement mechanical and the freeze wall must emit the complete node requirement set
gap: bounded-e3-to-e2-handback — e3 proved its destructive callers compose the shared authority verifier, then found that the verifier accepted missing/unknown epoch schema and missing/tampered lineage; main settled and preserved the first e3 attempt, opened exactly one e2 replay at nonce `5558d54a8213`, and folded e3 back to pending without changing the authored plan hash, so this dependency edge may not replay again in epoch 3
